recipes.name: Nasi Lemak
instructions: Wash rice and cook with coconut milk and pandan leaves until fluffy
Hard boil eggs and cut in half
Slice cucumber into thin rounds
Fry peanuts until golden and crispy
Fry ikan bilis until crispy
Prepare sambal or use store-bought
Plate rice and arrange egg, cucumber, peanuts, ikan bilis around it
Serve with sambal on the side

recipes.name: Char Kway Teow
instructions: Loosen flat rice noodles by rinsing with hot water
Slice chinese sausage diagonally
Mince garlic and cut chives into 2-inch lengths
Heat wok over high heat with oil
Stir-fry garlic until fragrant, add prawns and sausage
Push ingredients to side, crack eggs and scramble
Add noodles, toss quickly with dark soy sauce
Add regular soy sauce, bean sprouts and chives
Toss everything together over high heat for 2 minutes
Serve immediately while hot

recipes.name: Rendang Ayam
instructions: Cut chicken into large pieces
Blend shallots, garlic, ginger, galangal, dried chili and turmeric into paste
Bruise lemongrass stalks
Heat oil and fry spice paste until fragrant (10 minutes)
Add chicken pieces, coat well with paste
Pour in coconut milk and add lemongrass
Add tamarind paste, palm sugar and salt
Simmer on low heat for 1.5 hours, stirring occasionally
Cook until sauce is thick and oil separates
Serve with steamed rice

recipes.name: Roti Canai
instructions: Mix flour, salt and sugar
Add egg, condensed milk and gradually add water
Knead until smooth and elastic (10 minutes)
Divide dough into 8 balls, coat with ghee
Rest dough for 30 minutes covered with damp cloth
Flatten each ball and stretch until paper thin on greased surface
Fold into square shape
Heat flat pan and cook roti on medium heat with ghee
Flip when golden brown spots appear
Serve with dhal or curry

recipes.name: Laksa
instructions: Cook rice noodles according to package instructions, drain
Hard boil eggs, peel and halve
Heat oil and fry laksa paste until fragrant (3 minutes)
Add fish stock and bring to boil
Add coconut milk and simmer for 10 minutes
Add prawns and tofu puffs, cook until prawns turn pink
Blanch bean sprouts in boiling water briefly
Divide noodles into bowls
Pour hot laksa soup over noodles
Top with egg, bean sprouts, coriander and lime wedge

recipes.name: Nasi Goreng Kampung
instructions: Use day-old rice for best results
Blend shallots, garlic, dried chili and belacan into paste
Cut chicken into small pieces
Fry ikan bilis until crispy, set aside
Heat oil and fry spice paste until fragrant
Add chicken, stir-fry until cooked
Push to side, crack eggs and scramble
Add rice, break up clumps, stir-fry for 3 minutes
Add kangkung and ikan bilis, toss well
Season with salt and serve hot

recipes.name: Rojak
instructions: Cut cucumber, pineapple and jicama into bite-sized pieces
Cut fried dough fritters into chunks
Blanch bean sprouts briefly in hot water
Crush peanuts coarsely
Mix tamarind paste, palm sugar, belacan and lime juice for sauce
Add a little water if sauce is too thick
Combine all fruits and vegetables in bowl
Pour rojak sauce over and toss well
Sprinkle crushed peanuts on top
Serve immediately

recipes.name: Hainanese Chicken Rice
instructions: Rub chicken with salt, stuff cavity with ginger and spring onion
Boil large pot of water, add chicken and simmer for 40 minutes
Remove chicken and plunge into ice water to stop cooking
Reserve chicken stock for rice
Fry minced ginger and garlic until fragrant
Add rice, coat with oil and aromatics
Pour in chicken stock and pandan leaves, cook rice
Chop chicken into pieces
Make ginger sauce with grated ginger, sesame oil and salt
Serve chicken with rice, cucumber slices and ginger sauce

recipes.name: Curry Mee
instructions: Hard boil eggs, peel and halve
Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces
Heat oil and fry curry paste until fragrant
Add chicken, stir-fry until half cooked
Pour in 1 liter water, bring to boil
Add coconut milk and simmer for 15 minutes
Add prawns and tofu puffs, cook until prawns are done
Cook noodles and vermicelli separately, drain
Blanch bean sprouts
Assemble bowl with noodles, pour curry soup, top with egg and laksa leaves

recipes.name: Mee Goreng Mamak
instructions: Boil noodles briefly, drain and set aside
Cut chicken into small pieces, slice tofu
Boil potato until tender, cut into cubes
Quarter tomato
Heat wok with oil over high heat
Fry chicken and prawns until cooked, set aside
Fry tofu until golden, set aside
Crack eggs, scramble and break into pieces
Add noodles, toss with tomato sauce, soy sauce and chili sauce
Add back chicken, prawns, tofu, potato, tomato and bean sprouts
Toss everything together for 2 minutes
Serve with lime wedge

recipes.name: Roti Telur
instructions: Mix flour, salt and sugar
Add 1 egg, condensed milk and gradually add water
Knead until smooth dough forms
Divide into 4 balls, coat with ghee
Rest for 20 minutes
Flatten each ball and stretch very thin on greased surface
Crack one egg in center of stretched dough
Fold dough over egg to enclose it
Cook on hot griddle with ghee until golden and crispy
Serve hot with dhal or sugar

recipes.name: Nasi Kerabu
instructions: Steep butterfly pea flowers in hot water to extract blue color
Cook rice with blue water for blue rice
Grill or fry fish until cooked
Toast coconut until golden and grind coarsely
Slice shallots thinly and fry until crispy
Prepare ulam (raw vegetables/herbs)
Hard boil eggs and quarter them
Blend lemongrass, ginger, shallots for sambal
Serve blue rice with fish, kerisik (toasted coconut), ulam, eggs
Drizzle with sambal and top with fried shallots

recipes.name: Bak Kut Teh
instructions: Blanch pork ribs in boiling water to remove impurities
Peel whole garlic cloves
Dry roast white pepper, star anise, cinnamon and cloves
Tie roasted spices in muslin cloth or tea bag
Boil 2 liters water, add ribs and spice bag
Add garlic cloves, dark soy sauce and rock sugar
Simmer on low heat for 2 hours until meat is tender
Add enoki mushrooms 10 minutes before serving
Season with light soy sauce to taste
Serve hot with steamed rice and you tiao (fried dough)

recipes.name: Apam Balik
instructions: Mix flour, baking powder, baking soda and sugar
Add egg and gradually add water while whisking
Rest batter for 20 minutes
Crush peanuts coarsely and mix with sugar
Heat non-stick pan and lightly grease
Pour batter to form thin pancake
When bubbles appear, sprinkle peanut-sugar mixture and corn
Add small cubes of butter on top
Fold pancake in half when edges are set
Cook until golden and crispy, serve warm

recipes.name: Assam Pedas
instructions: Clean fish and cut into steaks
Blend shallots, garlic, dried chili, ginger and belacan
Bruise lemongrass stalks
Cut tomatoes into quarters
Heat oil and fry spice paste until fragrant
Add 500ml water and lemongrass
Add tamarind paste and sugar, bring to boil
Add fish pieces gently
Add okra and tomatoes
Simmer for 15 minutes until fish is cooked
Adjust seasoning with salt
Serve hot with steamed rice

recipes.name: Hokkien Mee
instructions: Rinse noodles and vermicelli with hot water
Slice pork belly thinly, clean and slice squid
Make prawn stock by boiling prawn heads and shells
Mince garlic
Heat wok over high heat with oil
Fry garlic until fragrant
Add pork, stir-fry until cooked
Add prawns and squid, cook until done
Add noodles and vermicelli, toss with dark soy sauce
Add prawn stock gradually while tossing
Add bean sprouts, toss for 1 minute
Serve with lime wedge and sambal

recipes.name: Cendol
instructions: Cook red beans until soft, sweeten with sugar
Mix rice flour with pandan juice into smooth batter
Cook batter over medium heat, stirring constantly until thick
Press cooked mixture through cendol mould into ice water
Boil palm sugar with water to make gula melaka syrup
Fill serving glasses with shaved ice
Add cendol, red beans and sweet corn
Pour coconut milk over
Drizzle with gula melaka syrup
Serve immediately

recipes.name: Murtabak
instructions: Make dough by mixing flour, water, salt and 1 egg
Knead until smooth, divide into balls and coat with ghee
Rest for 30 minutes
Dice onions finely, chop spring onions
Cook minced meat with curry powder, onions and salt
Stretch each dough ball very thin on greased surface
Place meat filling in center, crack egg over it
Sprinkle spring onions
Fold dough into square to enclose filling
Cook on hot griddle with ghee until golden and crispy
Cut into pieces and serve with curry or pickles

recipes.name: Popiah
instructions: Julienne jicama, carrot and cabbage finely
Dice tofu into small cubes, fry until golden
Crush peanuts coarsely
Mince garlic and stir-fry in oil
Add jicama, carrot and cabbage, stir-fry until soft
Season with salt and pepper
Lay out popiah skin, spread sweet sauce
Place lettuce leaf on skin
Add cooked vegetables, tofu, bean sprouts
Sprinkle crushed peanuts
Add chili sauce if desired
Roll tightly and cut diagonally to serve

recipes.name: Teh Tarik
instructions: Boil water in pot
Add black tea leaves and boil for 3 minutes
Strain tea into jug
Add condensed milk and evaporated milk
Pour tea between two mugs from height to create froth (pull the tea)
Repeat pulling action 7-8 times until frothy
Serve hot

recipes.name: Sayur Lodeh
instructions: Cut cabbage into chunks, slice long beans into 2-inch pieces
Cut carrot and eggplant into bite-sized pieces
Cut tofu into cubes
Blend shallots, garlic, dried chili and turmeric into paste
Bruise lemongrass and galangal
Heat oil and fry spice paste until fragrant
Add 500ml water, lemongrass and galangal, bring to boil
Add carrot and long beans, simmer for 5 minutes
Add cabbage, eggplant and tofu
Pour in coconut milk, simmer for 10 minutes
Season with salt and sugar
Serve with steamed rice

recipes.name: Gado-Gado
instructions: Boil potato until tender, slice
Blanch cabbage, bean sprouts and long beans separately
Slice cucumber thinly
Fry tofu until golden, slice
Roast peanuts until fragrant, grind coarsely
Blend peanuts, dried chili, garlic, palm sugar and tamarind paste
Add water gradually to make thick peanut sauce
Cook peanut sauce over low heat for 5 minutes, stirring
Arrange vegetables and tofu on plate
Pour peanut sauce over
Garnish with fried shallots
Serve immediately

recipes.name: Acar
instructions: Cut cucumber, carrot into matchsticks
Shred cabbage finely, cut long beans into 1-inch pieces
Roast peanuts until golden
Blend dried chili, shallots, garlic and turmeric into paste
Heat oil and fry spice paste until fragrant
Add vinegar, sugar and salt, bring to boil
Add all vegetables and peanuts
Stir-fry for 3 minutes (vegetables should remain crunchy)
Remove from heat and let cool
Transfer to jar and refrigerate
Serve as side dish or condiment

recipes.name: Kuih Lapis
instructions: Mix rice flour, tapioca flour and salt
Heat coconut milk, water and sugar until dissolved
Pour into flour mixture, whisk until smooth
Divide batter into 2 portions
Add pandan juice to one portion (green), food coloring to other (pink)
Grease steaming pan with oil
Pour thin layer of green batter, steam for 3 minutes
Add thin layer of pink batter, steam for 3 minutes
Repeat alternating colors until all batter is used
Steam final layer for 5 minutes
Cool completely before cutting into diamond shapes
Serve at room temperature

recipes.name: Onde-Onde
instructions: Mix glutinous rice flour with pandan juice and water to form soft dough
Chop palm sugar into small pieces
Steam grated coconut with pandan leaves and salt for 10 minutes
Take small portion of dough, flatten into disc
Place palm sugar piece in center
Wrap dough around sugar and roll into smooth ball
Bring large pot of water to boil
Drop onde-onde balls into boiling water
When balls float to surface, cook for 2 more minutes
Remove and drain
Roll in steamed coconut until well coated
Serve warm or at room temperature

recipes.name: Satay Ayam
instructions: Cut chicken into 1-inch cubes
Blend lemongrass, shallots, garlic, turmeric, coriander and cumin
Marinate chicken with spice paste for 2 hours
Thread chicken onto bamboo skewers
Grill over charcoal or in oven until cooked and slightly charred
For peanut sauce: roast peanuts and blend coarsely
Blend dried chili, shallots and garlic
Cook blended paste with peanuts, coconut milk, palm sugar and tamarind
Simmer until thick
Serve satay with peanut sauce, cucumber and rice cakes

recipes.name: Nasi Kandar
instructions: Cook rice with ghee and pandan leaves
Cut chicken into large pieces
Blend shallots, garlic, ginger and dried chili into paste
Heat oil and fry curry paste with curry leaves and curry powder
Add chicken, coat well with paste
Add water and coconut milk, bring to boil
Add tamarind paste, simmer for 20 minutes
In separate pan, fry okra and eggplant with curry paste
Serve rice with chicken curry and vegetables
Drizzle mixed curries over rice

recipes.name: Mee Rebus
instructions: Boil sweet potato until soft, mash
Blend shallots, garlic, dried chili and dried shrimp
Hard boil eggs, quarter them
Fry tofu until golden, slice
Heat oil and fry spice paste until fragrant
Add 1 liter water, bring to boil
Add mashed sweet potato, tamarind paste and palm sugar
Cook prawns in gravy until done
Simmer until thick and creamy
Blanch noodles and bean sprouts
Serve noodles with gravy, egg, tofu and fried shallots

recipes.name: Ikan Bakar
instructions: Clean fish and make diagonal cuts on both sides
Rub fish with turmeric and salt
Blend shallots, garlic, ginger, lemongrass, dried chili and belacan
Mix paste with tamarind paste and palm sugar
Rub spice paste all over fish and inside cuts
Marinate for 30 minutes
Wrap fish in banana leaf or foil
Grill over charcoal or in oven for 15-20 minutes, turning once
Unwrap and grill directly for 2 minutes for char
Serve with lime wedges and sambal

recipes.name: Roti Jala
instructions: Mix flour, turmeric powder and salt
Beat eggs lightly
Add eggs, coconut milk and water to flour
Whisk until smooth and no lumps remain
Rest batter for 15 minutes
Heat non-stick pan over medium heat, lightly grease
Pour batter into squeeze bottle or roti jala mould
Drizzle batter in circular lacy pattern on pan
Cook for 1 minute until set (do not flip)
Remove and fold into triangle
Repeat with remaining batter
Serve with curry or rendang

recipes.name: Bubur Ayam
instructions: Poach chicken in stock with ginger until cooked, about 20 minutes
Remove chicken and shred into pieces
Add washed rice to the stock
Simmer on low heat, stirring occasionally until rice breaks down into congee
Add more water if needed for desired consistency
Season with soy sauce, salt and white pepper
Mince garlic and fry until golden
Chop spring onions
Ladle congee into bowls
Top with shredded chicken, fried garlic, fried shallots and spring onion
Drizzle with sesame oil and serve hot

recipes.name: Nasi Tomato
instructions: Blend tomatoes into puree
Slice onions, mince garlic and ginger
Heat ghee, fry cinnamon and star anise until fragrant
Add onions, fry until golden
Add garlic and ginger, fry for 2 minutes
Add curry powder and tomato paste, stir well
Add blended tomatoes, cook for 5 minutes
Add washed rice and coat with tomato mixture
Pour in chicken stock, season with salt
Cook rice until done
Separately, marinate chicken in curry powder and fry until cooked
Serve tomato rice with fried chicken and acar

recipes.name: Ayam Pandan
instructions: Cut chicken thighs into bite-sized pieces
Blend lemongrass, garlic and shallots into paste
Mix paste with turmeric, coriander, oyster sauce, soy sauce and sugar
Marinate chicken in spice paste for at least 1 hour
Wrap each piece of marinated chicken in pandan leaf
Secure with toothpick
Heat oil in deep pan to 180°C
Deep fry pandan-wrapped chicken for 8-10 minutes until cooked
Drain on paper towels
Serve with sweet chili dipping sauce

recipes.name: Masak Lemak Cili Api
instructions: Cut chicken into pieces
Blend bird eye chili, shallots, garlic and galangal into paste
Bruise lemongrass
Heat coconut milk in pot over medium heat
Add blended paste, lemongrass and turmeric powder
Stir well and bring to gentle simmer
Add chicken pieces
Add young jackfruit if using
Simmer for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally
Season with salt
Serve hot with steamed rice

recipes.name: Otak-Otak
instructions: Blend lemongrass, shallots, garlic, dried chili, galangal, turmeric and belacan
Mince fish finely or pulse in food processor
Mix fish with spice paste, coconut milk and eggs
Shred kaffir lime leaves finely and mix in
Season with salt and sugar
Soften banana leaves by passing over flame briefly
Place 2 tablespoons of fish mixture on banana leaf
Fold leaf into parcel and secure with toothpick
Grill over charcoal or in oven for 15 minutes
Serve hot as appetizer or side dish

recipes.name: Ayam Percik
instructions: Halve or quarter chicken
Blend lemongrass, shallots, garlic, ginger and dried chili
Mix paste with turmeric, coriander, tamarind, palm sugar and salt
Coat chicken thoroughly with marinade
Marinate for at least 2 hours or overnight
Grill chicken on medium heat, turning occasionally
While grilling, heat coconut milk with remaining marinade
Baste chicken with coconut milk mixture during grilling
Continue grilling until chicken is cooked and caramelized
Serve with steamed rice, cucumber and tomato

recipes.name: Sambal Tumis Udang
instructions: Soak dried chili in hot water for 15 minutes, drain
Blend soaked chili, shallots, garlic and belacan
Shell and devein prawns, leave tails intact
Heat oil in wok over medium heat
Fry blended paste until fragrant and oil separates (10 minutes)
Add tamarind paste and palm sugar
Stir and cook for 2 minutes
Add prawns, stir-fry until cooked and coated with sambal
Season with salt
Serve hot with steamed rice

recipes.name: Dhal Curry
instructions: Rinse lentils and boil until soft, about 20 minutes
Dice onion and tomatoes
Mince garlic and ginger
Heat oil and add mustard seeds until they pop
Add curry leaves, onion and fry until golden
Add garlic and ginger, fry for 2 minutes
Add tomatoes, turmeric, cumin and coriander powder
Cook until tomatoes soften
Add boiled lentils and stir well
Simmer for 10 minutes, adding water for desired consistency
Season with salt
Garnish with fresh coriander and serve with roti or rice

recipes.name: Fish Head Curry
instructions: Clean fish head thoroughly
Blend onion, garlic, ginger and dried chili
Heat oil and fry curry leaves until fragrant
Add blended paste and fish curry powder
Fry until oil separates and paste is fragrant
Add 500ml water and tamarind paste
Bring to boil
Add fish head carefully
Add okra, tomato and eggplant
Pour in coconut milk
Simmer for 20 minutes until fish is cooked
Season with salt and serve with steamed rice

recipes.name: Idli with Sambar
instructions: Soak rice and urad dal separately for 6 hours
Blend soaked rice and dal separately, then combine
Ferment batter overnight
Pour batter into greased idli moulds
Steam for 10-12 minutes until firm
For sambar: boil lentils until soft
Fry mustard seeds and curry leaves in oil
Add onions and tomatoes, cook until soft
Add sambar powder, tamarind and lentils
Add drumstick pieces, simmer for 15 minutes
Season with salt
Serve hot idli with sambar and coconut chutney

recipes.name: Puri with Potato Curry
instructions: Mix flour with salt and water to make firm dough
Knead well, rest for 15 minutes
Boil potatoes until tender, peel and cube roughly
Fry mustard seeds and curry leaves
Add onion, fry until golden
Add garlic, ginger and tomato, cook until soft
Add turmeric and cubed potato
Mash some potato into gravy for thickness
Simmer curry for 10 minutes, season with salt
Divide dough into small balls, roll into circles
Deep fry puri in hot oil until puffed and golden
Serve hot puri with potato curry

recipes.name: Chicken Tikka
instructions: Cut chicken breast into large cubes
Mince garlic and ginger
Mix yogurt with garlic, ginger and all spices
Add lime juice and oil
Marinate chicken in yogurt mixture for at least 2 hours
Thread chicken onto skewers
Grill on high heat, turning occasionally
Cook until chicken is charred on edges and cooked through
Rest for 5 minutes
Garnish with fresh coriander and lime wedges
Serve with mint chutney and naan

recipes.name: Wonton Noodle Soup
instructions: Mince prawns and mix with minced meat
Season filling with soy sauce, sesame oil and white pepper
Place 1 teaspoon filling in center of each wonton wrapper
Wet edges with water and fold into triangle, press to seal
Bring chicken stock to boil with ginger and garlic
Simmer stock for 15 minutes and season with soy sauce
Cook noodles separately, drain
Boil wontons in stock until they float, about 5 minutes
Blanch bok choy in stock briefly
Divide noodles into bowls
Pour stock over with wontons and bok choy
Garnish with spring onion and sesame oil

recipes.name: Yong Tau Foo
instructions: Cut tofu, bitter gourd, eggplant and chili into pieces
Stuff fish paste into tofu pockets, bitter gourd rings and chili
Stuff eggplant and okra with fish paste
Bring chicken stock to boil with garlic
Add stuffed items to stock and simmer for 15 minutes
Remove stuffed items when cooked
Season stock with soy sauce
Arrange yong tau foo in bowl
Ladle hot soup over
Drizzle with sesame oil
Garnish with spring onion
Serve with chili sauce

recipes.name: Claypot Chicken Rice
instructions: Soak shiitake mushrooms until soft, slice
Cut chicken thighs into pieces
Slice chinese sausage diagonally
Marinate chicken with dark soy sauce, oyster sauce and sesame oil for 30 minutes
Rinse rice and place in claypot
Add water and bring to boil on stove
When water is absorbed, create wells in rice
Arrange marinated chicken, sausage and mushrooms on rice
Cover and cook on low heat for 20 minutes
Drizzle dark soy sauce around edges for crispy bottom
Garnish with spring onion
Serve directly from claypot

recipes.name: Steamed Fish with Soy Sauce
instructions: Clean fish, make diagonal cuts on both sides
Place ginger slices inside fish cavity and on top
Steam fish on high heat for 10-12 minutes
While fish steams, julienne remaining ginger and spring onion
Mix soy sauce with a little sugar
Remove steamed fish, discard water
Pour soy sauce mixture over fish
Top with julienned ginger, spring onion and chili
Heat oil and garlic until smoking
Pour hot oil over fish (it will sizzle)
Garnish with fresh coriander
Serve immediately with steamed rice

recipes.name: Beef Rendang
instructions: Cut beef into large chunks
Toast grated coconut until dark brown to make kerisik
Blend shallots, garlic, ginger, galangal, dried chili and turmeric
Bruise lemongrass stalks
Heat oil and fry spice paste until fragrant
Add beef and coat well
Pour in coconut milk and add lemongrass and kaffir lime leaves
Add tamarind paste and salt
Simmer on low heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally
Add kerisik when sauce starts to thicken
Continue cooking until sauce is almost dry and meat is very tender
Serve with steamed rice or ketupat

recipes.name: Kuih Seri Muka
instructions: Soak glutinous rice for at least 2 hours
Steam rice with coconut milk and salt until cooked
Press cooked rice firmly into greased pan
For pandan custard layer: mix pandan juice with eggs and sugar
Add coconut milk, flour and tapioca flour
Cook over low heat, stirring constantly until thick
Pour pandan custard over rice layer
Steam for 20-25 minutes until custard is set
Cool completely before cutting
Cut into diamond shapes with wet knife
Serve at room temperature

recipes.name: Cucur Udang
instructions: Shell and devein prawns
Slice shallots thinly, chop spring onion
Mix all purpose flour, rice flour, turmeric and salt
Add egg and water, whisk into smooth batter
Fold in prawns, shallots, spring onion and bean sprouts
Heat oil in wok or deep pan to 180°C
Drop spoonfuls of batter into hot oil
Fry until golden brown on both sides, about 3 minutes
Drain on paper towels
Serve hot with chili dipping sauce

recipes.name: Asam Laksa
instructions: Boil mackerel in 1.5 liters water until cooked
Remove fish, flake flesh and discard bones
Return stock to pot
Blend lemongrass, dried chili, shallots, galangal and belacan
Fry blended paste until fragrant
Add fish stock and bring to boil
Add tamarind paste and flaked fish
Simmer for 20 minutes
Julienne cucumber and slice pineapple
Slice onion into thin rings
Cook noodles, drain and divide into bowls
Pour hot laksa soup over noodles
Top with cucumber, pineapple, onion, mint and hae ko

recipes.name: Sup Tulang
instructions: Blanch beef bones in boiling water for 10 minutes, drain and rinse
Place bones in large pot with 2 liters of water
Add whole spices: star anise, cinnamon and cloves
Add roughly chopped onion, garlic and ginger
Bring to boil then simmer on low heat for 1.5 hours
Skim off any foam that rises to surface
Cut potato and carrot into chunks
Add potato and carrot to soup, simmer for 20 minutes
Season with salt to taste
Garnish with spring onion and fried shallots
Serve hot with bread or rice

recipes.name: Nasi Dagang
instructions: Soak both types of rice together for 2 hours
Steam rice with pandan leaves until half cooked
Mix coconut milk with fenugreek seeds and salt
Pour coconut milk over rice, steam until fully cooked
Clean and cut tuna into large chunks
Blend shallots, garlic, ginger and dried chili into paste
Heat oil and fry spice paste until fragrant
Add tuna pieces carefully
Add tamarind paste and palm sugar
Simmer for 20 minutes until sauce thickens
Serve nasi dagang with tuna curry and acar

recipes.name: Lontong
instructions: Cook rice until done, press firmly into banana leaf rolls
Tie rolls tightly and refrigerate overnight until firm
Slice compressed rice rolls into rounds
Blend shallots, garlic, dried chili and turmeric
Fry spice paste with lemongrass and galangal until fragrant
Add 500ml water and bring to boil
Add coconut milk and simmer for 10 minutes
Cut vegetables and add carrot and long beans first
Add cabbage after 5 minutes
Fry tofu until golden and add to curry
Season with salt
Serve rice rolls with vegetable coconut curry

recipes.name: Pecal
instructions: Blanch bean sprouts, kangkung and long beans separately until just tender
Slice cucumber into thin pieces
Fry tofu until golden, slice into pieces
Roast peanuts until fragrant
Blend peanuts with dried chili, garlic and belacan
Add tamarind paste, palm sugar and a little water
Cook peanut sauce over low heat until thick
Arrange all vegetables and tofu on plate
Pour peanut sauce generously over
Serve immediately

recipes.name: Soto Ayam
instructions: Boil chicken with lemongrass and ginger until cooked
Remove chicken and shred the meat
Keep the stock for soup base
Blend shallots, garlic and turmeric into paste
Fry spice paste until fragrant
Add chicken stock and coriander powder, bring to boil
Simmer for 15 minutes, season with salt
Hard boil eggs, peel and halve
Boil potato until tender, slice
Cook rice vermicelli, drain
Assemble bowl with vermicelli, shredded chicken, egg and potato
Pour hot soup over, serve with lime and sambal

recipes.name: Gulai Ikan
instructions: Clean fish and cut into steaks
Blend shallots, garlic, ginger, galangal, dried chili and turmeric
Bruise lemongrass stalks
Heat oil and fry spice paste with lemongrass until fragrant
Add 200ml water and bring to boil
Pour in coconut milk and stir well
Add tamarind paste
Gently add fish pieces
Add okra
Simmer for 15 minutes until fish is cooked
Season with salt
Serve with steamed rice

recipes.name: Ayam Goreng Berempah
instructions: Cut chicken into pieces
Blend shallots, garlic, ginger, lemongrass and dried chili
Mix blended paste with turmeric, coriander, cumin and salt
Add coconut milk to marinade
Coat chicken thoroughly with marinade
Marinate for at least 2 hours, preferably overnight
Heat oil in deep pan to 170°C
Deep fry chicken in batches until deep golden brown
Cook for 12-15 minutes until fully cooked through
Drain on paper towels
Serve hot with steamed rice and sambal

recipes.name: Bubur Lambuk
instructions: Wash rice and place in large pot with 2 liters water
Bring to boil and simmer, stirring frequently
Blend shallots, garlic and ginger
Fry spice paste with lemongrass, coriander and cumin
Add chicken pieces and cook through
Shred chicken into fine pieces
Add chicken and spice mixture to rice porridge
Add dried shrimp
Pour in coconut milk and stir well
Simmer until porridge is thick and creamy
Season with salt
Serve topped with fried shallots and spring onion

recipes.name: Nasi Ulam
instructions: Cook rice and allow to cool completely
Toast grated coconut until golden brown
Dry fry dried shrimp until fragrant, pound coarsely
Slice lemongrass, kaffir lime leaves and turmeric leaf very finely
Slice shallots thinly
Mix cooled rice with all sliced herbs
Add toasted coconut and pounded dried shrimp
Make sambal belacan by blending belacan and dried chili with lime juice
Toss rice mixture well
Season with salt to taste
Serve with sliced cucumber and sambal belacan

recipes.name: Serunding Daging
instructions: Boil beef until tender, about 45 minutes
Shred beef into fine strands
Toast grated coconut until golden to make kerisik
Blend lemongrass, shallots, garlic, ginger, galangal and dried chili
Heat oil and fry spice paste until fragrant
Add shredded beef and stir well
Add turmeric, coriander, palm sugar and tamarind paste
Add kerisik and stir to combine
Cook over low heat, stirring frequently
Continue until mixture is dry and dark brown
Season with salt
Serve as condiment with rice or ketupat

recipes.name: Sambal Belacan
instructions: Soak dried chili in hot water for 15 minutes until soft
Toast belacan on dry pan until fragrant
Drain soaked chili
Pound or blend dried chili, bird eye chili, belacan and shallots
Pound to coarse paste (not too smooth)
Add lime juice
Season with salt and sugar
Taste and adjust seasoning
Serve as condiment with rice dishes

recipes.name: Masak Lemak Cili Padi Sotong
instructions: Clean squid, remove ink sac and cut into rings
Blend bird eye chili, shallots, garlic and galangal
Bruise lemongrass
Heat coconut milk in pot over medium heat
Add blended paste, lemongrass and turmeric
Stir and bring to gentle simmer
Add squid rings
Cut long beans and add to pot
Simmer for 12 minutes until squid is cooked
Do not overcook squid or it becomes tough
Season with salt
Serve hot with steamed rice

recipes.name: Prawn Noodle Soup
instructions: Peel prawns, keep heads and shells for stock
Fry prawn heads and shells until bright red
Add water and dried shrimp, simmer for 30 minutes
Strain to get rich prawn stock
Season stock with soy sauce and salt
Hard boil eggs, peel and halve
Blanch noodles and vermicelli separately
Blanch bean sprouts briefly
Cook prawns in hot stock until pink
Divide noodles into bowls
Pour hot prawn stock over noodles
Top with prawns, egg, bean sprouts and spring onion

recipes.name: Chee Cheong Fun
instructions: Mix rice flour with water until smooth
Add salt and vegetable oil, stir well
Grease a flat tray or steaming pan
Pour thin layer of batter onto tray
Steam for 4-5 minutes until set and translucent
While hot, roll up the rice sheet
Repeat with remaining batter
Fry shallots until crispy
Mix soy sauce with sweet sauce
Drizzle sauces over rolled rice noodles
Top with fried shallots, sesame seeds and spring onion
Drizzle sesame oil and serve

recipes.name: Tau Fu Fah
instructions: Heat soy milk to just below boiling point
Mix gypsum powder and tapioca flour with a little cold water
Pour hot soy milk quickly into container with gypsum mixture
Do not stir, cover immediately
Allow to set for 15-20 minutes undisturbed
For ginger syrup: boil water with palm sugar, pandan and ginger
Simmer syrup for 10 minutes, strain
Scoop silky tofu pudding into bowls
Pour warm ginger syrup over
Serve warm or chilled

recipes.name: Sang Har Meen
instructions: Deep fry egg noodles until crispy and golden
Place crispy noodles on serving plate
Clean prawns, keep shells on for flavor
Fry garlic and ginger in hot wok
Add prawns and stir-fry until almost cooked
Add 300ml water, oyster sauce and soy sauce
Bring to boil
Mix cornstarch with water, add to thicken gravy
Beat eggs and swirl into gravy
Season and add sesame oil
Pour gravy with prawns over crispy noodles
Garnish with spring onion and serve immediately

recipes.name: Steamed Egg
instructions: Beat eggs lightly without creating too much foam
Add lukewarm chicken stock to eggs
Season with salt and mix gently
Strain mixture through fine sieve
Pour into steaming bowl
Cover bowl with cling wrap or plate
Steam on low heat for 12-15 minutes
Check doneness by inserting toothpick (should come out clean)
Remove from steamer carefully
Drizzle soy sauce and sesame oil
Top with chopped spring onion
Serve immediately with steamed rice

recipes.name: Braised Tofu
instructions: Cut tofu into thick slices or cubes
Pan fry tofu in oil until golden on both sides
Remove tofu and set aside
Mince garlic and ginger
Fry garlic and ginger until fragrant
Add 200ml water, soy sauce, oyster sauce and sugar
Bring to boil
Dissolve cornstarch in water and add to thicken sauce
Return tofu to pan
Coat tofu with sauce and simmer for 5 minutes
Drizzle sesame oil
Garnish with spring onion and serve

recipes.name: Chicken Biryani
instructions: Marinate chicken with yogurt, half the spices, ginger and garlic for 2 hours
Slice onions thinly and fry until deep golden brown (birista)
Par boil basmati rice until 70% cooked, drain
Cook marinated chicken with tomatoes until half done
Layer in large pot: rice, chicken, fried onions, mint leaves
Repeat layers
Dissolve saffron in warm milk and drizzle on top
Add ghee on top layer
Seal pot tightly with foil or dough
Cook on low heat (dum) for 25 minutes
Mix gently from bottom when serving
Serve with raita and acar

recipes.name: Tandoori Chicken
instructions: Score chicken pieces deeply with knife
Rub with salt and lime juice, rest for 20 minutes
Blend garlic and ginger into paste
Mix yogurt with all spices, garlic-ginger paste and oil
Coat chicken thoroughly with marinade, pushing into cuts
Marinate for at least 4 hours or overnight
Preheat oven to 220°C or grill to high
Grill or bake chicken turning occasionally
Cook until charred at edges and cooked through
Rest for 5 minutes
Squeeze lime juice over
Garnish with coriander, serve with naan and mint chutney

recipes.name: Naan
instructions: Mix flour with baking powder, salt and sugar
Add yogurt, egg, oil and warm water
Knead until smooth and soft dough forms
Cover and rest for 45 minutes
Divide dough into 6 equal balls
Roll each ball into oval shape
Heat cast iron pan or tawa until very hot
Cook naan on dry pan until bubbles appear
Flip and cook other side
Melt butter with minced garlic
Brush hot naan with garlic butter
Garnish with coriander and serve

recipes.name: Palak Paneer
instructions: Blanch spinach in boiling water for 2 minutes
Cool spinach in cold water and blend into smooth puree
Cut paneer into cubes and fry until golden
Dice onions and chop tomatoes
Fry onions until golden brown
Add garlic and ginger, cook for 2 minutes
Add tomatoes and cook until pulpy
Add cumin, coriander and garam masala
Add spinach puree and stir well
Simmer for 10 minutes
Add fried paneer cubes
Stir in cream, season and serve with naan

recipes.name: Mutton Curry
instructions: Cut mutton into large chunks
Blend garlic and ginger into paste
Slice onions and chop tomatoes
Heat oil and fry onions until deep brown
Add garlic-ginger paste and curry leaves
Add all spice powders and fry for 2 minutes
Add tomatoes and cook until oil separates
Add mutton pieces and coat with masala
Add 500ml water and pressure cook for 30 minutes
Or simmer in pot for 60-75 minutes until tender
Add coconut milk and garam masala
Simmer for 10 more minutes
Serve with rice or roti

recipes.name: Prawn Masala
instructions: Shell and devein prawns
Slice onions, chop tomatoes
Mince garlic and ginger
Heat oil and add curry leaves
Fry onions until golden
Add garlic and ginger, cook for 2 minutes
Add tomatoes and cook until soft
Add turmeric, coriander, chili powder, mix well
Add prawns and stir to coat with masala
Pour in coconut milk
Cook for 8-10 minutes until prawns are done
Sprinkle garam masala and coriander, serve

recipes.name: Vegetable Kurma
instructions: Cube potato and carrot, boil until just tender
Soak cashew nuts in warm water for 20 minutes, blend smooth
Slice onions, chop tomatoes
Fry onions until golden
Add garlic, ginger and tomato
Add coriander powder and cook until fragrant
Add cashew paste and stir well
Add coconut milk and 200ml water
Add potato, carrot and green peas
Simmer for 15 minutes
Add garam masala, season with salt
Garnish with coriander and serve with roti

recipes.name: Chapati
instructions: Mix flour and salt in bowl
Add oil and rub into flour
Gradually add water and knead until soft dough forms
Knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth and pliable
Cover with damp cloth and rest for 20 minutes
Divide dough into 10 equal balls
Roll each ball into thin circle on floured surface
Heat tawa or flat pan on high heat
Cook chapati until small bubbles appear, flip
Press edges gently with cloth to help puffing
Both sides should have brown spots
Apply butter if desired, serve warm

recipes.name: Rasam
instructions: Cook lentils until soft
Chop tomatoes and crush garlic
Mix tamarind paste with 2 cups water
Add tomatoes, lentils, turmeric and black pepper to tamarind water
Simmer for 15 minutes
Heat oil in separate pan
Add mustard seeds until they pop
Add cumin seeds, curry leaves and garlic
Fry until fragrant
Pour tempering into rasam
Simmer for 5 more minutes
Garnish with coriander, serve as soup or with rice

recipes.name: Masala Dosa
instructions: Soak rice and urad dal separately for 6 hours
Grind rice and dal separately to smooth batter
Mix together, ferment overnight
For filling: boil potatoes until soft, peel and mash roughly
Fry mustard seeds and curry leaves in oil
Add sliced onions, green chili and ginger
Add turmeric and mashed potato, mix well
Season filling with salt and coriander
Heat griddle, grease lightly
Pour ladleful of batter and spread in circular motion
Add filling in center, fold dosa over
Cook until crispy, serve with sambar and chutney

recipes.name: Aloo Gobi
instructions: Cut potato and cauliflower into bite-sized pieces
Slice onion, chop tomato
Mince garlic and ginger
Heat oil and add cumin seeds until they sizzle
Add onion and fry until golden
Add garlic and ginger, cook for 2 minutes
Add tomato and cook until soft
Add turmeric and coriander powder
Add potato and cauliflower, stir to coat
Cover and cook on medium heat for 15 minutes
Stir occasionally until vegetables are tender
Add garam masala, garnish with coriander and serve

recipes.name: Butter Chicken
instructions: Marinate chicken with yogurt, half the spices and garlic-ginger paste
Grill or bake chicken until slightly charred
For sauce: blend onion, tomato, garlic and ginger
Melt butter in pan, add blended mixture
Cook over medium heat until thick and oil separates
Add remaining spices and cook for 3 minutes
Add 200ml water and bring to boil
Add grilled chicken pieces
Pour in cream and stir gently
Simmer for 10 minutes
Add garam masala, season with salt
Garnish with coriander and serve with naan or rice

recipes.name: Char Siu Pork
instructions: Slice pork belly into long strips about 3cm thick
Mix honey, soy sauce, oyster sauce, dark soy sauce, sugar and five spice
Add minced garlic, sesame oil and food coloring
Marinate pork in sauce for at least 4 hours or overnight
Preheat oven to 200°C
Place pork on rack over baking tray
Roast for 20 minutes, baste with marinade
Flip and roast another 15 minutes
Baste again and roast 5 more minutes until caramelized
Rest for 5 minutes before slicing
Serve with steamed rice and blanched vegetables

recipes.name: Lo Mee
instructions: Blanch yellow noodles in hot water, drain
Slice chicken thinly, shell and devein prawns
Mince garlic and fry until fragrant
Add chicken and prawns, stir-fry until cooked
Add chicken stock and bring to boil
Season with dark soy sauce and oyster sauce
Mix cornstarch with water and add to thicken gravy
Beat eggs and swirl into gravy
Add bean sprouts and stir briefly
Add vinegar for tanginess
Place noodles in bowl and pour thick gravy over
Serve immediately with chili and lime

recipes.name: Pak Choy with Oyster Sauce
instructions: Wash and halve bok choy lengthwise
Blanch in boiling salted water for 2 minutes
Drain and arrange on serving plate
Mince garlic finely
Heat oil in small pan
Fry garlic until golden and fragrant
Mix oyster sauce, soy sauce, sugar and cornstarch with 3 tbsp water
Pour sauce mixture into pan with garlic
Cook until sauce thickens slightly
Drizzle sesame oil
Pour sauce over blanched bok choy
Serve immediately

recipes.name: Kuey Teow Soup
instructions: Poach chicken in stock until cooked, shred meat
Return stock to pot and bring to boil
Add garlic, soy sauce and white pepper to stock
Season with salt to taste
Cook noodles separately and drain
Blanch bean sprouts briefly
Add prawns to hot stock and cook until pink
Divide noodles into bowls
Ladle hot soup with chicken and prawns over noodles
Top with bean sprouts and spring onion
Drizzle sesame oil
Garnish with fried shallots and serve

recipes.name: Kuih Bahulu
instructions: Whisk eggs and sugar together until pale and fluffy (8 minutes)
Sift flour and baking powder together
Fold flour mixture gently into egg mixture
Add vanilla extract and oil
Fold until just combined, do not overmix
Preheat bahulu mould over flame until hot
Grease mould well with oil
Fill each mould cavity two-thirds full
Close mould and cook over medium flame
Turn mould over after 2 minutes
Cook until golden and firm, about 4-5 minutes total
Tap out and cool on rack

recipes.name: Pulut Kuning
instructions: Soak glutinous rice overnight in water with turmeric powder
Drain and rinse rice
Mix coconut milk with salt
Steam rice with pandan leaves for 20 minutes
Pour half coconut milk over rice, mix well
Continue steaming for 15 more minutes
Add remaining coconut milk, steam until fully cooked
Toast grated coconut with sugar until golden
Press cooked rice firmly into mould or tray
Allow to set for 30 minutes
Cut into diamond shapes
Serve with toasted coconut and beef rendang

recipes.name: Mee Siam
instructions: Soak rice vermicelli until soft, drain
Blend dried shrimp, shallots, garlic and dried chili
Fry tofu until golden, slice
Hard boil eggs, halve
Heat oil and fry spice paste until fragrant
Add 600ml water, tamarind paste and palm sugar
Bring to boil, season with salt
Add prawns and cook until done
Add vermicelli and toss to coat with sauce
Add bean sprouts and toss briefly
Top with fried tofu, halved eggs and lime
Serve with sambal

recipes.name: Kerabu Mangga
instructions: Peel green mango and julienne or grate into thin strips
Slice shallots thinly
Pound dried shrimp coarsely
Toast grated coconut until golden
Pound bird eye chili and belacan together
Mix lime juice, palm sugar and pounded chili paste
Taste and adjust sweetness and sourness
Combine mango, shallots and dried shrimp
Pour dressing over and toss well
Top with toasted coconut
Serve as salad or side dish

recipes.name: Karipap
instructions: Mix flour with salt, rub in butter until crumbly
Add water gradually and knead to soft dough
Rest dough covered for 20 minutes
Dice potato, mince chicken
Fry onion until soft, add curry powder and turmeric
Add chicken and potato, cook until done
Season filling with salt, allow to cool
Divide dough into small balls
Roll each ball into circle
Place filling in center, fold and crimp edges
Deep fry in batches until golden brown
Drain on paper towels and serve warm

recipes.name: Nasi Impit
instructions: Wash rice thoroughly until water runs clear
Cook rice with water, pandan leaves and salt
Cook until fully done and slightly overcooked
Remove pandan leaves
While rice is still hot, press firmly into rectangular container
Pack rice tightly with back of spoon or wet hands
Cover with plastic wrap and press down firmly
Allow to cool completely at room temperature
Refrigerate for at least 2 hours until set solid
Unmould onto cutting board
Cut into diamond or cube shapes with wet knife
Serve with satay and peanut sauce

recipes.name: Sup Ekor
instructions: Blanch oxtail in boiling water for 10 minutes, drain and rinse
Place oxtail in pressure cooker or large pot with 2 liters water
Add whole spices: star anise, cinnamon, cloves and black pepper
Add roughly chopped onion, garlic and ginger
Pressure cook for 45 minutes or simmer for 2.5 hours until tender
Skim off excess fat from surface
Cut potato and carrot into chunks
Add potato and carrot, cook for 20 minutes until tender
Season generously with salt
Ladle into deep bowls
Garnish with spring onion and fried shallots
Serve hot with bread or steamed rice

recipes.name: Udang Galah Masak Lemak
instructions: Clean prawns and trim whiskers if desired
Blend bird eye chili, shallots, garlic and galangal
Bruise lemongrass stalks
Pour coconut milk into pot over medium heat
Add blended paste, lemongrass and turmeric
Add pandan leaves tied in knot
Stir and bring to gentle simmer
Add prawns carefully
Cook for 10-12 minutes until prawns are done
Season with salt
Remove pandan leaves before serving
Serve hot with steamed rice

recipes.name: Tom Yam Ayam
instructions: Cut chicken into bite-sized pieces
Bruise lemongrass and slice galangal
Tear kaffir lime leaves
Boil 1 liter water with lemongrass, galangal and kaffir lime leaves
Simmer for 10 minutes to infuse flavors
Add chicken and cook until done
Add sliced mushrooms and tomatoes
Add bird eye chili and fish sauce
Pour in coconut milk
Simmer for 5 minutes
Squeeze in lime juice
Garnish with coriander and serve hot

recipes.name: Pais Ikan
instructions: Clean fish and slice into pieces
Blend lemongrass, shallots, garlic, dried chili, galangal and belacan
Mix paste with turmeric powder
Combine fish with spice paste and coconut milk
Shred kaffir lime leaves finely and add
Season with salt
Soften banana leaves briefly over flame
Place fish mixture on banana leaf portion
Wrap into neat parcel and secure with toothpick
Steam parcels for 20 minutes
Alternatively grill for 15 minutes each side
Serve in banana leaf with steamed rice

recipes.name: Kuih Talam
instructions: Mix rice flour and tapioca flour
Divide flour mixture into two equal portions
For green layer: mix one portion with pandan juice and half the sugar
For white layer: mix other portion with coconut milk, salt and remaining sugar
Strain both batters separately
Grease steaming tray
Pour green pandan layer first
Steam for 12 minutes until set
Carefully pour white coconut layer on top
Steam for another 15 minutes until set
Cool completely before cutting
Cut into squares and serve at room temperature

recipes.name: Spicy Tofu Stir Fry
instructions: Cut tofu into cubes and pat dry
Pan fry tofu until golden on all sides
Remove and set aside
Mince garlic and ginger
Stir fry garlic and ginger until fragrant
Add chili sauce and cook for 1 minute
Mix soy sauce, sugar and cornstarch with 3 tbsp water
Add sauce mixture to pan
Return tofu and toss to coat
Simmer until sauce thickens
Drizzle sesame oil
Garnish with spring onion and serve

recipes.name: Egg Fried Rice
instructions: Use day-old cold rice for best results
Break up any rice clumps
Beat eggs lightly with pinch of salt
Mince garlic, chop spring onion
Heat wok over high heat until smoking
Add oil and fry garlic until fragrant
Add rice and stir-fry breaking up clumps
Push rice to side, pour eggs into center
Scramble eggs then mix with rice quickly
Add soy sauce and white pepper
Toss everything together for 2 minutes
Drizzle sesame oil, garnish with spring onion

recipes.name: Chicken Congee
instructions: Rinse rice and soak for 30 minutes
Bring chicken stock to boil
Add rice and sliced ginger
Simmer on low heat stirring occasionally
Add chicken pieces after 20 minutes
Continue simmering for 30 minutes until rice breaks down
Remove chicken and shred
Return shredded chicken to congee
Fry garlic until golden
Season congee with soy sauce, salt and white pepper
Ladle into bowls
Top with fried garlic, fried shallots, spring onion and sesame oil

recipes.name: Kuih Dadar
instructions: Mix flour with pandan juice, coconut milk and eggs
Whisk into smooth green batter
For filling: cook palm sugar with water and pandan leaves
Add grated coconut and cook until dry and caramelized
Remove pandan leaves and cool filling
Heat non-stick pan over medium heat, lightly grease
Pour small ladle of batter and swirl into thin crepe
Cook until set, do not flip
Place 1 tablespoon of coconut filling along center
Roll crepe tightly over filling
Repeat with remaining batter and filling
Serve at room temperature

recipes.name: Nasi Ambeng
instructions: Cook rice and mound on large banana leaf lined tray
Blend shallots, garlic, dried chili and belacan for sambal
Fry sambal paste until fragrant and oil separates
Add chicken pieces to sambal with coconut milk
Add tamarind and palm sugar, simmer until cooked
Slice tempeh and fry until golden
Fry salted fish until crispy
Stir fry long beans and cabbage with garlic
Season all dishes with salt
Arrange chicken sambal, tempeh, salted fish and vegetables around rice
Traditionally served on communal tray for sharing
Eat together from shared plate with hands
